_comment: Allowlisted npm advisories. Every entry: {"id": <advisory id or GHSA>, "reason": "...", "owner": "...", "expires": "YYYY-MM-DD"}. Must match a row in docs/operating/SECURITY_GATES.md.

allowlist.reason: HIGH in sharp pulled in by next; npm fix is a semver-major DOWNGRADE to next 14. Real fix arrives when next bumps its bundled sharp. An npm override would force a lockfile regen, prohibited on Windows (strips linux binaries; sharp is a native module). Expiry forces a re-check.

APPLIES — PRIORITY ITEM. Middleware/proxy bypass; precondition is Turbopack + single locale, and the prod build log reads 'Next.js 16.2.10 (Turbopack)' with no i18n config. Impact is bounded: frontend/middleware.ts only SETS response headers and always returns NextResponse.next() — it performs no authorization. A bypass therefore serves /embed/:token without its per-widget CSP frame-ancestors (defence-in-depth from PR #167), but grants no data access: the widget token is the access control and is validated backend-side. No stable fixed release exists (vulnerable range ends at 16.3.0-preview.7). Re-check on expiry; exit is stable 16.3.0.

NOT EXPOSED — Denial of Service in App Router Server Actions. The repo contains zero 'use server' directives; no Server Actions are defined. Allowlisted because the gate requires every advisory on a flagged package to be keyed, not because the risk was accepted.
NOT EXPOSED — SSRF in Server Actions on custom servers. No Server Actions exist (zero 'use server'), and the app runs on 'next start', not a custom server.

APPLIES, MODERATE — cache confusion of response bodies for requests with bodies. Generic to the framework; no stable fixed release exists. Rated moderate by the advisory; the package-level HIGH that blocks the gate comes from the middleware and rewrite advisories, not this one.
APPLIES, MODERATE — cache confusion variant for request bodies containing invalid UTF-8 byte sequences. Same posture as 1124186.
NOT EXPOSED — unbounded Server Action payload in the Edge runtime. No Server Actions exist (zero 'use server').

NOT EXPOSED — SSRF in rewrites via an attacker-controlled destination hostname. The only rewrite is /api/backend/:path* -> ${BACKEND_INTERNAL}/:path* (next.config.ts). The destination hostname is fixed from environment configuration; only the path segment is caller-supplied, which is outside this advisory's vector.
APPLIES, MODERATE — DoS in the Image Optimization API via SVGs. Surface is minimal: a single next/image usage (app/components/UserAvatar.tsx). No stable fixed release exists.
NOT EXPOSED — unauthenticated disclosure of internal Server Function endpoints. No Server Functions or Server Actions exist in the repo (zero 'use server').
